gaps.requirement: Cross-AI delivery — api RECEIVING half (Shape 1a): the compose-validation + outcome-report endpoint pair the extension presents the identity-minted delivery token to. api validates composedTextRef by hash equality and RESERVES the token on validate; SPENDS it and writes the ONE authoritative RYW-relevant delivery audit on outcome-report.
gaps.quote: SHAPE 1a — api compose-VALIDATION + outcome-report calls (seam, codegen'd) ... api validates presented.composedTextRef == token.composedTextRef by HASH EQUALITY ONLY ... RESERVES the token without yet marking it spent and without yet writing the audit. The token is spent, and the ONE authoritative RYW-relevant audit entry written ... only on the SEPARATE outcome-report call (ViWUZj1MrW §8).
gaps.why_uncovered: identity ENG-2448 (E10-S4) owns ONLY the AD-8 mint (Shape 2) and explicitly defers the compose/report behaviour as 'api/ui-owned'. The extension stories ENG-2711..2730 own only the client — they PRESENT the token and REPORT the outcome (Shapes 1b/emit). api E4-S2 (ENG-2311) is the Composer egress audit, a different surface. Grep of all 575 story bodies: zero contain `composedTextRef` or `outcome-report`. The api receiving endpoints were never storied.
gaps.verdict: CONFIRMED — source ViWUZj1MrW canonical; grep of feature-map/breakdown/fix-specs confirms api never implements Shape 1a; blocked_by correct (own pack ENG-2098 + Wave-1 seam ENG-2091/2037 + extension pack ENG-2690). Note: near-duplicate proposals independently exist in sibling ext-sweep/architecture.json and contract-spec.json — pipeline must dedupe before ticket creation.

proposed_issue.title: [api] Cross-AI delivery server half — Shape 1a compose-validation + outcome-report endpoints (token reserve→spend, composedTextRef hash-equality, the one authoritative delivery audit + RYW SoR)
proposed_issue.milestone: NEW: B — Cross-AI delivery server half (compose-validation, outcome-report, canary ingest)
proposed_issue.body_md:
## 🎯 Story

As **the browser extension delivering enriched Memory into a third-party assistant**, I want **an api-side Shape 1a compose-validation + outcome-report endpoint pair** so that **the extension can present the identity-minted delivery token (Shape 2/AD-8), have api validate the exact consented bundle by content-hash and RESERVE the token, then on the terminal delivery report SPEND the token and write the one authoritative delivery audit — closing the content-swap-after-mint hole and satisfying the read-your-writes guarantee.**

**Definition of Done:** ONE named test `TestDeliveryValidateReservesThenOutcomeReportSpends` (integration) — asserts (a) `validate` refuses when `presented.composedTextRef != token.composedTextRef` by HASH EQUALITY (never re-derives/re-serves the text), (b) a valid `validate` RESERVES the token without spending it or writing the audit, (c) `outcome-report {injected|copied|aborted}` SPENDS the token exactly once and writes the single authoritative RYW-relevant audit entry, and (d) a GO-provider canary-fail-to-`copied` reports the SAME token's outcome, never a second mint. *Dispatch-blocked until the api Definition-Pack contract tag (ENG-2098) and the Wave-1 contracts seam tag exist.*

## ✅ Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] **AC1** — Given a presented delivery token + `composedTextRef`, When `validate` runs, Then api returns `{validated}` only if `presented.composedTextRef == token.composedTextRef` by hash equality, else `{refused}` — never re-serving or re-deriving the text. *Assert: mismatched hash → refused; matched → validated.* [Source: ViWUZj1MrW §8 Shape 1a]
- [ ] **AC2** — Given a `{validated}` result, When it returns, Then the token is RESERVED (not spent) and NO audit is written yet. *Assert: post-validate token state == reserved; audit table unchanged.* [Source: ViWUZj1MrW §8 Shape 1a, AD-EXT-3]
- [ ] **AC3** — Given the client reports terminal outcome, When `outcome-report {deliveryToken, outcome}` is called, Then api SPENDS the token once and writes exactly ONE authoritative RYW-relevant delivery audit entry. *Assert: double outcome-report → single spend, single audit (idempotent).* [Source: ViWUZj1MrW §8 Shape 1a, AD-EXT-6/AD-EXT-7]
- [ ] **AC4** — Given a DEGRADE provider (Claude/Grok) or a GO canary-fail, When the client reports `copied`, Then it spends the same reserved token via the same outcome-report — never a second mint. *Assert: copied path reuses the reserved token; no re-mint.* [Source: ViWUZj1MrW §4]
- [ ] **AC5 (ordering)** — Given the AD-EXT-3 invariant, When `validate` has not returned `{validated}`, Then the client's Shape 1b DOM outcome is never produced (api decides VALIDITY only, never `{ok|needs-manual-paste}`). *Assert: no outcome-report accepted without a prior reserving validate for that token.* [Source: ViWUZj1MrW §8 Shape 1a/1b]
- [ ] **AC6 (RYW read)** — Given the extension reads the RYW SoR, When it queries api after a just-confirmed card, Then the served value is never stale-past-that card. *Assert: RYW SoR read reflects the latest confirmed Memory (AD-EXT-7).* [Source: ViWUZj1MrW §3, AD-11]

## 🔨 Tasks
- [ ] RED: `TestDeliveryValidateReservesThenOutcomeReportSpends` covering hash-equality refuse, reserve-not-spend, spend-once + single audit, copied-reuse (AC1–4).
- [ ] GREEN: `validate` endpoint — present token, hash-equality check on `composedTextRef`, reserve token, no audit write (AC1–2).
- [ ] GREEN: `outcome-report` endpoint — spend token idempotently, write the one authoritative delivery audit entry (AC3–4).
- [ ] Enforce AD-EXT-3 ordering (validate-before-outcome) + RYW SoR read surface for the extension (AC5–6).
- [ ] Consume the Shape 1a contract via the generated TS client / server stubs off the contracts seam (no hand-rolled types).

## 🧠 Context
**🧾 Gap provenance (2026-07-15):** filed from the ratified extension canon sweep — the Cross-AI memory-loop end-to-end audit of the six now-canonical extension pages (ViWUZj1MrW §3/§4/§8, g9vWbSYplh F1). Verified UNOWNED: no story body across the 575-story corpus contains `composedTextRef`, `outcome-report`, or an api-side canary ingest; identity ENG-2448 owns only the AD-8 mint (Shape 2), extension ENG-2711..2730 own only the client (Shapes 1b/emit); the api receiving half was never storied.

Tier: api application endpoint pair (delivery-token lifecycle) → consent/audit domain (reuses E3-S3 consent + E1-S6 audit stream + E4-S2/ENG-2311 egress-audit machinery, DISTINCT surface). This is the api RECEIVING half of Cross-AI delivery: identity mints (Shape 2, ENG-2448), the extension presents + reports (client, ENG-2711..2730), api validates + reserves + spends + audits (this story). The composed text is fetched by-value once from knowledge/ai (§4 SW→K) — served there, not here; api validates the REFERENCE (composeId/content-hash) only.

## 🧪 Testing
- Named DoD test: `TestDeliveryValidateReservesThenOutcomeReportSpends` (integration).
- Tiers: unit (hash-equality, token state machine) + integration (reserve→spend→audit, idempotency). CS §5: drive real token/audit stores; stub the client and the knowledge/ai by-value fetch at their edges.

## 📏 Guidance
CS `6aMAzsYeQb` §§0/3/4/5/6/10/11 (consent chokepoint, single-audit, content-swap defense, security). SE-Arch `8sYi523i4t`: Security lens (content swap after mint; token replay) + invariant-consistency (validate never returns a DOM outcome). cell-lint `JGAUQRsw2g` (audit events cell-stamped).

## 🔗 References
Extension arch `ViWUZj1MrW` §3/§4/§8 (Shape 1a) · PRD `g9vWbSYplh` F1 (FR-D1/FR-D4/FR-D5) · identity AD-8 mint `ENG-2448` · api Private-Memory egress audit `ENG-2311`.

## 🔗 Dependencies
- [ ] Blocked by: **ENG-2098** (api Definition Pack — contract TAG = dispatch gate).
- [ ] Blocked by: **ENG-2091** + **ENG-2037** (Wave-1 contracts seam — Shape 1a lands here; both Todo).
- [ ] Blocked by: **ENG-2690** (extension Definition Pack — authors Shape 1a/1b/2/3 in §8).
- [ ] Sibling/cross-service: **ENG-2448** (identity Shape 2 mint), **ENG-2711..2730** (extension client presenter/reporter).

## 📡 Protocol
CLAIM → PLAN → PROGRESS → COMMITS ("Part of ENG-NNN", never closes) → HANDOFF → REVIEW (reviewer ≠ implementer) → TICK → DONE (orchestrator-only) → ESCALATE.

gaps.requirement: Cross-AI delivery — api breakage-canary telemetry INGEST endpoint (Shape 3): the sole Orvex service that ingests the extension's canary heartbeat and re-emits it as an OTLP metric (canary_trip_total) + a structured Loki log line driving the paging alert, never as a CloudEvent.
gaps.quote: The service worker POSTs the classified event (Shape 3 ...) to orvex-studio-api's canary-telemetry ingest endpoint over the SAME generated TS client used for the compose call — api is the sole ingesting Orvex service; there is no separate telemetry service to invent. api re-emits the event as an OTLP metric (canary_trip_total{provider,class,selectorPackVersion}) and a structured Loki log line ... it is never re-emitted as a CloudEvent (ViWUZj1MrW §3/§8 Shape 3).
gaps.why_uncovered: The canary/breakage telemetry appears only in EXTENSION bodies (E2-S3/E5-S2/E5-S3 — the emit side). No api body mentions a canary/telemetry ingest endpoint. §3 names api the sole ingester ('no separate telemetry service to invent') yet no api story stands up the receiving endpoint or the OTLP/Loki re-emit + paging-alert wiring.
gaps.verdict: CONFIRMED — source ViWUZj1MrW §3/§8 canonical and quote verified verbatim; no api story ingests the canary; blocked_by correct (ENG-2098/2091/2037/2690). Note: same underlying gap independently proposed in sibling ext-sweep/architecture.json (as one delivery-seam story) — dedupe before ticket creation.

proposed_issue.title: [api] Breakage-canary telemetry ingest endpoint (Shape 3) — OTLP metric canary_trip_total + Loki log + per-(provider,version,class) paging alert, never a CloudEvent
proposed_issue.milestone: NEW: B — Cross-AI delivery server half (compose-validation, outcome-report, canary ingest)
proposed_issue.body_md:
## 🎯 Story

As **the operations team paged when a provider silently changes its web UI**, I want **an api-side breakage-canary telemetry ingest endpoint (Shape 3)** so that **the extension service worker can POST a classified canary trip over the same contracts seam, and api re-emits it as an OTLP metric + a structured Loki log line that drives the paging alert — never as a CloudEvent (a canary trip is operational health, not a memory-domain state change).**

**Definition of Done:** ONE named test `TestCanaryIngestReEmitsOtlpAndLokiNotCloudEvent` (integration) — POSTs a Shape 3 event `{providerId, selectorPackVersion, failureClass, tier, timestamp}` and asserts api (a) emits the OTLP metric `canary_trip_total{provider,class,selectorPackVersion}`, (b) writes the structured Loki log line that drives the alert, (c) fires a DISTINCT alert per `(provider + selectorPackVersion + failureClass)`, and (d) NEVER publishes it onto the CloudEvent/studio-spine bus (CS §10 non-conflation). *Dispatch-blocked until the api Definition-Pack contract tag (ENG-2098) and the Wave-1 contracts seam tag exist.*

## ✅ Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] **AC1** — Given a Shape 3 canary POST, When ingested, Then api emits the OTLP metric `canary_trip_total{provider,class,selectorPackVersion}`. *Assert: metric emitted with the exact labels.* [Source: ViWUZj1MrW §3/§8 Shape 3, FR-BC5]
- [ ] **AC2** — Given the same POST, When ingested, Then api writes the structured Loki log line that drives the paging alert. *Assert: log line present + parseable; alert rule keys off it.* [Source: ViWUZj1MrW §3]
- [ ] **AC3 (non-conflation)** — Given a canary trip, When ingested, Then it is NEVER re-emitted as a CloudEvent on studio-spine. *Assert: zero CloudEvents produced by the ingest path.* [Source: ViWUZj1MrW §3, CS §10]
- [ ] **AC4 (alert granularity)** — Given trips across versions/classes, When ingested, Then a distinct alert fires per `(provider + selectorPackVersion + failureClass)`. *Assert: two classes on one provider → two alerts.* [Source: ViWUZj1MrW §3]
- [ ] **AC5 (sole ingester)** — Given the client cannot reach a collector directly (AD-EXT-5 zero non-Orvex egress), When it reports, Then api is the sole ingesting Orvex service over the generated TS client. *Assert: endpoint reachable only over the contracts-seam client; no separate telemetry service.* [Source: ViWUZj1MrW §3, AD-EXT-5]
- [ ] **AC6 (validation)** — Given a malformed/foreign `failureClass` (not in {NOTFOUND,NOTWRITABLE,AMBIGUOUS,VERIFY}), When POSTed, Then it is rejected typed, not ingested. *Assert: bad class → 4xx, no metric/log.* [Source: ViWUZj1MrW §8 Shape 3]

## 🔨 Tasks
- [ ] RED: `TestCanaryIngestReEmitsOtlpAndLokiNotCloudEvent` covering OTLP + Loki + no-CloudEvent + per-tuple alert (AC1–4).
- [ ] GREEN: canary-telemetry ingest endpoint accepting Shape 3 over the generated TS client (AC5).
- [ ] GREEN: re-emit as OTLP metric `canary_trip_total{...}` + structured Loki log; wire the paging alert rule per `(provider+version+class)` (AC1,2,4).
- [ ] Enforce failure-class allow-list validation + typed rejection (AC6); explicit non-conflation guard (no CloudEvent path) (AC3).

## 🧠 Context
**🧾 Gap provenance (2026-07-15):** filed from the ratified extension canon sweep — the Cross-AI memory-loop end-to-end audit of the six now-canonical extension pages (ViWUZj1MrW §3/§4/§8, g9vWbSYplh F1). Verified UNOWNED: no story body across the 575-story corpus contains `composedTextRef`, `outcome-report`, or an api-side canary ingest; identity ENG-2448 owns only the AD-8 mint (Shape 2), extension ENG-2711..2730 own only the client (Shapes 1b/emit); the api receiving half was never storied.

Tier: api operational-telemetry endpoint → OTel/Loki (reuses E1-S6/E8-S5-style observability wiring, DISTINCT surface — this is the extension's canary sink, not memory-domain audit). Pairs with the extension emit side (ENG-2725 selector fixtures + heartbeat, ENG-2726 fail-loud + team alert). §3 names api the SOLE ingesting service — "there is no separate telemetry service to invent."

## 🧪 Testing
- Named DoD test: `TestCanaryIngestReEmitsOtlpAndLokiNotCloudEvent` (integration).
- Tiers: unit (class validation, label shaping) + integration (OTLP/Loki emit, no-CloudEvent assertion). CS §5: assert against a real OTel/Loki test exporter; stub the client edge.

## 📏 Guidance
CS `6aMAzsYeQb` §§10/11 (event non-conflation — telemetry ≠ CloudEvent; honest fail-loud) · §0 security (untrusted client input validated). SE-Arch `8sYi523i4t`: invariant-consistency (a canary trip is operational health, never a memory state change). cell-lint `JGAUQRsw2g`.

## 🔗 References
Extension arch `ViWUZj1MrW` §3 (canary-telemetry routing) / §8 Shape 3 · PRD `g9vWbSYplh` F1 (FR-D7 self-breakage detection) · CS `6aMAzsYeQb` §10 non-conflation.

## 🔗 Dependencies
- [ ] Blocked by: **ENG-2098** (api Definition Pack — contract TAG = dispatch gate).
- [ ] Blocked by: **ENG-2091** + **ENG-2037** (Wave-1 contracts seam — Shape 3 lands here; both Todo).
- [ ] Blocked by: **ENG-2690** (extension Definition Pack — authors Shape 3 in §8).
- [ ] Sibling/cross-service: **ENG-2725**, **ENG-2726** (extension canary emit + fail-loud/team-alert).

## 📡 Protocol
CLAIM → PLAN → PROGRESS → COMMITS ("Part of ENG-NNN", never closes) → HANDOFF → REVIEW (reviewer ≠ implementer) → TICK → DONE (orchestrator-only) → ESCALATE.

gaps.requirement: AI-privacy leg — the per-vendor training/retention posture registry BACKEND that serves the ui AI-privacy surface (ENG-2697) via the BFF, with honest unknown/unsupported states, scoped to third-party connected assistants and never conflated with Orvex's own no-train config.
gaps.quote: Consume the BFF per-vendor posture registry contract (must-resolve backend share ...). Must-resolve (pack review): the backend share — a per-vendor training/retention posture registry (likely the ai service) — is an open pack-review must-resolve; this story consumes it and is flagged pending that ruling (ENG-2697 / fix-ai-privacy).
gaps.why_uncovered: ENG-2697 (ui) RENDERS the posture from a BFF registry but explicitly flags the backend as an open pack-review must-resolve ('likely the ai service'). Grep of all 575 story bodies for a vendor training/retention posture registry returns zero — no ai/api story owns it. Distinct from ENG-2696 (knowledge outbound-sync adapters, which push memory OUT but do not describe vendor posture) and from Orvex's own no-train config (workgraph NFR-MEM7). Left 'TBD — pinned at pack review'; still unowned.
gaps.verdict: CONFIRMED — quote verified against fix-ai-privacy.md (ENG-2697) verbatim; grep confirms no ai/api story owns a vendor posture registry; FIXED a real defect: proposed_issue.blocked_by wrongly listed ENG-2697 as a blocker when ENG-2697 is actually blocked BY/consumes this new story (body_md's own Dependencies section already said 'Consumed by / blocks: ENG-2697') — removed ENG-2697 from blocked_by, left ENG-2097 (ai's own Definition Pack).

proposed_issue.title: [ai] Per-vendor training/retention posture registry backend — server-truth source for the ui AI-privacy surface (honest unknown states, never conflated with Orvex own-provider config)
proposed_issue.milestone: NEW: B — Connected-assistant posture registry (serves ui AI-privacy)
proposed_issue.body_md:
## 🎯 Story

As **the ui AI-privacy surface (ENG-2697) verifying a connected assistant won't train on the user's data**, I want **an ai-service per-vendor training/retention posture registry backend** so that **the BFF has a server-truth source for each connected vendor's training/retention posture — with an explicit `unknown/unsupported` state when a vendor's posture can't be verified — instead of the SPA scraping vendor policies or fabricating a "safe" claim.**

**Definition of Done:** ONE named test `TestVendorPostureRegistryServesTruthWithHonestUnknown` (integration) — asserts the backend (a) serves a per-vendor training/retention posture record for a known vendor, (b) returns an explicit `unknown`/`unsupported` posture (never a fabricated affirmative) for an unverifiable vendor, (c) reflects a posture change on the next read (server-truth, no stale cached affirmative), and (d) is scoped to THIRD-PARTY connected assistants only — never conflated with Orvex's own no-train provider config (workgraph NFR-MEM7) or the api Private-Memory guard. *Dispatch-blocked until the ai Definition-Pack contract tag (ENG-2097) and the pack-review ownership ruling (below) land.*

## ✅ Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] **AC1** — Given a known connected vendor, When the BFF requests its posture, Then the registry serves that vendor's training/retention posture record. *Assert: posture value served from registry state, not hard-coded.* [Source: g9vWbSYplh, ENG-2697 AC1]
- [ ] **AC2 (honest unknown)** — Given an unverifiable/unreported vendor, When queried, Then the registry returns an explicit `unknown`/`unsupported` posture, never a fabricated affirmative. *Assert: unknown vendor → unsupported; no "safe" claim.* [Source: ENG-2697 AC2/AC5]
- [ ] **AC3 (server-truth)** — Given a vendor posture changes, When the registry updates, Then the next read serves the new posture (no stale cached affirmative). *Assert: post-update read reflects the change.* [Source: ENG-2697 AC4]
- [ ] **AC4 (non-conflation)** — Given the registry, When it serves, Then it is scoped to third-party connected assistants only and never returns/derives from Orvex's own no-train provider config (NFR-MEM7) or the api Private-Memory guard. *Assert: no coupling to own-provider config or api guard.* [Source: ENG-2697 AC3, workgraph NFR-MEM7]
- [ ] **AC5** — Given the beachhead vendors (ChatGPT, Claude, Gemini, Grok), When seeded, Then each has a posture record (or an explicit `unknown`), sourced from a maintainable posture-catalog, not per-request scraping. *Assert: all four beachhead vendors resolvable; source is catalog, not live scrape.* [Source: g9vWbSYplh FR-D3]
- [ ] **AC6 (negative)** — Given a vendor absent from the catalog, When queried, Then the backend returns `unsupported` (falls safe), never invents a posture. *Assert: absent vendor → unsupported, no fabricated record.* [Source: ENG-2697 AC5]

## 🔨 Tasks
- [ ] RED: `TestVendorPostureRegistryServesTruthWithHonestUnknown` across known / unverifiable / distinct-from-Orvex / absent vendors (AC1–4,6).
- [ ] GREEN: per-vendor training/retention posture registry store + read API the BFF consumes (AC1,5).
- [ ] GREEN: honest `unknown`/`unsupported` state + server-truth re-read semantics (AC2,3); non-conflation scoping vs own-provider config and the api guard (AC4).
- [ ] Seed the beachhead-vendor posture catalog with a maintainable update path (AC5); absent-vendor falls-safe guard (AC6).
- [ ] **Pack-review must-resolve:** confirm ai (vs api) owns this registry — carried open from ENG-2697; resolve at ai pack certification (ENG-2097).

## 🧠 Context
**🧾 Gap provenance (2026-07-15):** filed from the ratified extension canon sweep. The ui AI-privacy surface (ENG-2697) explicitly consumes "the BFF per-vendor posture registry" and flags, verbatim, `Must-resolve (pack review): the backend share — a per-vendor training/retention posture registry (likely the ai service) — is an open pack-review must-resolve`. That backend was left 'TBD — pinned at pack review'; a grep of all 575 story bodies for a vendor training/retention-posture registry returns zero — no ai/api story owns it. Live loose end.

Tier: ai backend registry/read-API → consumed by the ui BFF (the SPA never scrapes vendor policies). Distinct from: Orvex's OWN no-train provider config (workgraph NFR-MEM7 — governs Orvex's own model providers), the api Private-Memory guard (internal memory egress), and the knowledge outbound-sync adapters (ENG-2696 — pushes memory OUT, does not describe vendor posture). This is the unowned backend half of the AI-privacy leg; ENG-2697 renders it, nothing serves it.

## 🧪 Testing
- Named DoD test: `TestVendorPostureRegistryServesTruthWithHonestUnknown` (integration).
- Tiers: unit (posture resolution, unknown/absent falls-safe) + integration (BFF-facing read API, server-truth re-read). CS §5: drive the real registry store; fixture the BFF consumer at its edge.

## 📏 Guidance
CS `6aMAzsYeQb` §11 (honest unknown/unsupported — no fabricated "safe") · §5 (fixture the sibling BFF) · §0 security. SE-Arch `8sYi523i4t`: honesty + invariant-consistency lens — the connected-assistant posture must never fork into or conflate with Orvex's own no-train config.

## 🔗 References
Brief `rgBOQh31p3` ("AI-privacy setup") · PRD `g9vWbSYplh` (F1 FR-D3) · ui consumer `ENG-2697` (per-vendor posture registry, flagged must-resolve) · workgraph NFR-MEM7 (distinct: Orvex OWN provider no-train config).

## 🔗 Dependencies
- [ ] Blocked by: **ENG-2097** (ai Definition Pack — contract TAG + the ai-vs-api ownership must-resolve = dispatch gate).
- [ ] Consumed by / blocks: **ENG-2697** (ui AI-privacy surface — currently fixtures this registry pending the backend).
- [ ] Related (distinct concern): **ENG-2696** (knowledge outbound-vendor sync adapters).

## 📡 Protocol
CLAIM → PLAN → PROGRESS → COMMITS ("Part of ENG-NNN", never closes) → HANDOFF → REVIEW (reviewer ≠ implementer) → TICK → DONE (orchestrator-only) → ESCALATE.
